scope: Public examples of GPU-kernel inference attribution issues.
note: This is a structured public-examples summary, not a full raw audit log.
stars_note: GitHub stars observed on 2026-07-01; stars are visibility context, not a quality score.
pattern: Many headline speedups can over-credit custom kernels when the optimized path also changes cache shape, graph capture, batching, precision, or compiler settings. The decisive measurement is the marginal custom-kernel gain over the best framework static-cache and torch.compile path.

public_examples.evidence: Optimized path includes compiled/static execution, so eager HF generate is not enough to isolate kernel contribution.
README credits CUDA graphs for the main 2-3x path, not isolated custom kernels.
The optimized path receives static cache and manual CUDA graph replay; the published torch.compile baseline is not the analogous StaticCache + reduce-overhead setup.
Its own decomposition reports Triton-only around 1.1x, Faster around 4x, and Hybrid around 5x.
Its own mode table reports Triton-only 1.02x and CUDA-Graph-only 2.75x.
Reported speedups require path-level benchmark audit before attributing gains to kernels alone.
Its own table reports a torch.compile + FlashAttention 2 path beating the custom-kernel path.
Precision regime and full-model-vs-microbenchmark attribution need to be read separately; reported full-model gain is around 1.1-1.2x.
